content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Counter-Bias Prompting via Real-Time Stereotype Detection and Adaptive Reframing",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently exhibit implicit social biases and stereotypes during generation, which can persist even with static debiasing prompts. Existing debiasing strategies often lack real-time, context-sensitive intervention at inference.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly integrated into high-stakes, socially sensitive applications, mitigating the propagation of bias is critical. Prior work primarily relies on static prompts, data-level interventions, or post-hoc analysis, which may not adapt to the evolving context or nuanced stereotypes in real user queries. There is a pressing need for prompting strategies that dynamically detect and correct bias during generation without retraining or heavy external supervision.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting framework that interleaves user queries with dynamic stereotype probes and real-time adaptive reframing. At each step, the model generates both the canonical response and a parallel ‘stereotype probe’ response targeted to expose potential bias through templates constructed on-the-fly using context-specific demographic cues. An auxiliary prompt then adaptively reframes the main response by conditionally incorporating outputs from the stereotype probe: if bias is detected (as measured by a lightweight classifier or rule-based system), the model is prompted to regenerate or revise its answer with explicit anti-bias instructions referencing the detected issue. This closed-loop mechanism operates entirely at inference time, without model fine-tuning.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on established bias benchmarks (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, WinoBias) and real-world QA datasets containing diverse demographic content. Baselines include zero-shot, instruction-tuned, and self-debiasing prompting. Effectiveness is measured by stereotype score reduction, answer quality (BLEU/ROUGE), and human evaluation for bias and utility."
}
